title: Guapira heterophylla
taxonomy: Guapira heterophylla is classified in the family Nyctaginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the four o'clock family. This family consists of about 30 genera and 900 species, mostly native to the Americas. Guapira heterophylla belongs to the genus Guapira, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Guapira is characterized by its shrub-like appearance and showy flowers. Guapira heterophylla is a species within the genus Guapira that is often found in tropical and subtropical forests.

intro: Guapira heterophylla, also known as Guapira or Guaparina, is a small evergreen shrub native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Guapira heterophylla include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Guapira heterophylla is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
